decisions.decision: Shift swap MVP is giveaway-only, no directed 1:1 swap
decisions.rationale: Stefan explicitly declined 1:1 swap — shifts aren't comparable enough to trade directly

decisions.decision: Hybrid approval by shift status: planned=immediate (compliance pre-check only), confirmed=admin/manager review required
decisions.rationale: Consistent with the existing rule that confirmed shifts can only be changed by admin/manager; avoids making Stefan a bottleneck for routine planned-shift handoffs

decisions.decision: Concurrency guard via conditional UPDATE (WHERE status='open') inside the same transaction as the compliance check, not SELECT FOR UPDATE
decisions.rationale: Simpler, works identically on SQLite (tests) and Postgres (prod) via row locking during the uncommitted transaction

decisions.decision: Celery tasks use a separate TaskSessionLocal/task_engine with NullPool + --pool=threads instead of the FastAPI app's pooled AsyncSessionLocal
decisions.rationale: asyncpg connections are bound to the event loop they were created on; each Celery task call starts its own asyncio.run() loop, and the default prefork pool additionally corrupts the connection via fork() — this was silently breaking every reminder task in production

decisions.decision: Money/Decimal rounding: verified already fixed in a prior session (money() helper + Numeric(10,2) columns + test_money_rounding.py) — did not do a redundant full Decimal migration
decisions.rationale: The real bug (rounding correctness) was already resolved; a full type migration would be pure churn with real risk (Decimal/float mixing, JSON serialization) for no remaining correctness benefit

decisions.decision: Developer-/Hilfe-Bereich scoped down to /help + Feedback module only, skipped the SuperAdmin-only versioned story-catalog tier from the original Fable concept
decisions.rationale: CLAUDE.md + the assistant's memory system already serve that role for developer-facing docs; an in-app YAML catalog would need constant upkeep for comparatively little ongoing value at VERA's scale

uncommitted_files: frontend/next-env.d.ts, frontend/tsconfig.tsbuildinfo (auto-generated build artifacts, dirty since before this session)

.claude/, ARCHITECTURE.md, IMPROVEMENTS.md, PROJEKT-ANALYSE-2026-07-06.md, ideas/, konzept-website-admin-blaupause.md, token-optimierung-modellvorauswahl.md (untracked since before this session — not part of this session's work, left untouched per user instruction)
next_action: Either (a) add the missing audit_service.write() calls for ESS-05 (shift acknowledge + availability update), (b) start Phase 5 (PWA/Mobile) or Phase 7 (RBAC audit), or (c) wait for Stefan to order the Hetzner Storage Box and then wire up the off-site backup rsync in deploy/backup.sh.
context_notes: This session ran a full Fable-5 project review, then implemented and deployed everything from the resulting priority list except the SuperAdmin story-catalog (explicitly scoped down) and the directed 1:1 shift swap (explicitly declined by Stefan). All work is deployed to production and verified (6/6 containers healthy, alembic head p0q1r2s3t4u5, 448 backend tests passing). Biggest find of the session: the Celery reminder pipeline had been silently broken in production since it was introduced — every run threw asyncpg.InterfaceError, swallowed by a try/except, so Celery reported success while never sending a single reminder.
